title: hieracium murorum subsp. seripodum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. seripodum, also known as wall hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving digestive issues. Hieracium murorum subsp. seripodum can be prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, or it can be used in tincture or salve form to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
